title: velaea toluccensis
intro: Velaea toluccensis, also known as the Mexican silver poppy or Velaea, is a flowering plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving coughs, and soothing irritated skin. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
